top5.reason: Portugal's NHR 2.0 flat 20% tax and D8 digital nomad visa are purpose-built for UK founders post-Brexit; Lisbon's growing tech scene and €3,000/month average cost sit comfortably within the £3,500 equivalent budget.

Estonia's e-Residency enables EU company formation with 0% corporate tax on retained earnings; digital-first infrastructure and flat 20% income tax directly serve the lowTax and techEco top priorities.

Georgia's Virtual Zone status grants 0% corporate tax on foreign-sourced IT revenue; UK passport enters visa-free for up to 365 days, the most generous no-cost window of any option, and Tbilisi costs ~$1,500/month.

Malta's ~5% effective corporate tax via the shareholder refund system and EU jurisdiction give a post-Brexit UK founder a compliant low-tax EU base; English is an official language eliminating the language barrier.
Cyprus offers 12.5% corporate tax, EU membership, and an English-speaking environment; warm Mediterranean climate and a straightforward non-dom regime round out the lowTax and visaEase priorities.
